summary: Russian forces launched a large‑scale UAV and missile attack across multiple fronts, shooting down 114 enemy drones while striking Kyiv, Kharkiv and oil infrastructure. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted many Shahed‑type drones and a pair of Iskander‑M missiles, but civilian casualties rose to at least 5 dead and 35 injured in Kyiv. Drone activity continues over the Black Sea, Moscow region and several Ukrainian oblasts.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine with two Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from Rostov region and 132 Shahed‑type UAVs, hitting nine locations; Ukrainian defenses shot down 114 UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
A Russian UAV struck the Kyiv‑Pechersk Lavra; components were traced to the Alabuga economic zone in Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81941).
Ukrainian forces hit an oil depot in the Poltavskaya stanitsa on the Kuban, a key logistics hub for Lukoil fuel distribution (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
Over 30 UAVs were destroyed in Kamensk‑Shakhtinsky and four districts of Rostov region (Source: @tass_agency/380850).
A Russian UAV caused a fire at the Moscow refinery, prompting emergency pressure reduction (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
Ukrainian air defenses downed a reactive Shahed approaching Kropyvnytskyi (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67813).
Russian forces liberated the village of Artema (Dolgaya Balka) in the Konstantinovka direction of the Donetsk People's Republic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30916).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces launched a coordinated strike with Iskander‑M missiles and over a hundred Shahed‑type UAVs, hitting multiple Ukrainian cities and oil facilities. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 114 drones and repelled the missile attack, but civilian casualties rose in Kyiv. Drone activity also affected Moscow, the Black Sea and several front‑line regions.

points: Ukrainian air defenses shot down 114 enemy UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Italmas types, across the north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
Ukrainian forces struck the oil depot in Poltavskaya stanitsa, Kuban, disrupting a key fuel hub for Lukoil (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
A reactive Shahed UAV approached Kropyvnytskyi, prompting alerts from local air defense (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67813).
Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on targets in Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @kpszsu/65273).
Ukrainian units reported a UAV near Hotin in Sumy region moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/65260).
Ukrainian forces engaged a UAV north of Sumy heading toward Chernihiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65291).
Russia launched two Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from Rostov region against Ukrainian targets (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
Russian UAVs caused a fire at the Moscow refinery, leading to emergency pressure reduction (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
A Russian UAV hit the Kyiv‑Pechersk Lavra; components were manufactured in the Alabuga zone (Source: @UkraineNow/81941).
Russian forces liberated the village of Artema (Dolgaya Balka) in the Konstantinovka direction of the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30916).
More than 30 UAVs were destroyed in Kamensk‑Shakhtinsky and four districts of Rostov region, indicating intensive Russian UAV deployment (Source: @tass_agency/380850).
A Russian drone damaged the Moscow oil refinery (MNPZ) with no casualties reported (Source: @tass_agency/380865).
UAVs were reported over Donetsk region heading toward Kramatorsk and Sloviansk (Source: @kpszsu/65306).
UAV activity was noted in Dnipropetrovsk region near Shakhtarske and Verkhivtseve, moving eastward (Source: @kpszsu/65301; @kpszsu/65305).
Russian attacks with Iskander‑M missiles targeted eastern Ukrainian locations as part of the 16‑missile strike (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
Ukrainian air defenses downed UAVs over the eastern front, contributing to the total of 114 shot down (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
UAVs were observed north of Sumy heading toward Chernihiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65291).
Russian UAV attacks were recorded in the north, with debris falling in eight locations (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
Ukrainian defenses intercepted multiple UAVs over the northern oblasts as part of the large‑scale air defense effort (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
No specific ground engagements were detailed for the northern front in the source material.
UAVs operated from Kherson region towards Mykolaiv, including locations such as Berzeneguvate (Source: @eRadarrua/96870).
Two controlled UAVs were spotted over the Black Sea near a cape, heading south (Source: @eRadarrua/96836).
A reactive UAV was reported on the border of Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk heading to Tomakivka (Source: @kpszsu/65284).
Ukrainian forces struck an oil depot in Poltavskaya stanitsa, Kuban, a southern logistics node (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 114 enemy UAVs of various types across the country (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
Russian forces launched two Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from Rostov region (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
Guided aerial bombs were deployed by Russian tactical aviation on Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @kpszsu/65273).
UAV attacks were reported over Moscow and its refinery, causing operational disruptions (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
UAV debris caused a fire at the oil depot in Poltavskaya stanitsa, Kuban (Source: @tass_agency/380857).

key_developments: Russia executed a coordinated strike with two Iskander‑M missiles from Rostov and 132 Shahed‑type UAVs, hitting nine locations; Ukrainian defenses downed 114 UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/65307).
A Russian UAV struck the historic Kyiv‑Pechersk Lavra; components traced to Alabuga economic zone in Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81941).
Ukrainian forces hit the Poltavskaya oil depot in Kuban, a key Lukoil logistics hub (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
Over 30 UAVs were destroyed in Kamensk‑Shakhtinsky and four districts of Rostov region, indicating heavy Russian UAV deployment (Source: @tass_agency/380850).
A reactive Shahed UAV approached Kropyvnytskyi, prompting local air defense alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67813).
Russian UAV debris caused a fire at a Moscow refinery, leading to emergency pressure reduction (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
Russian forces liberated the village of Artema (Dolgaya Balka) in the Konstantinovka direction of the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30916).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and UAV operations demonstrate a sustained capability to strike deep into Ukrainian territory and critical infrastructure, including energy hubs and cultural sites. Ukrainian air defenses remain effective, intercepting the majority of UAVs, but the volume of attacks strains detection and response assets. Continued Russian UAV activity over the Black Sea and southern fronts threatens maritime logistics and coastal installations.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing air defense layers in the north, east and south, deploying mobile fire groups and electronic warfare units to counter UAV swarms. International partners have supplied additional air defense systems, though shortages of Patriot missiles persist. Russian attacks on logistics nodes aim to disrupt fuel supplies to the southern front, prompting Ukrainian counter‑strikes on those facilities.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch points in Rostov and Kherson regions is critical, as patterns suggest coordinated swarm tactics. The use of reactive Shahed drones near Kropyvnytskyi indicates a shift to low‑altitude threats against inland targets. Satellite imagery should focus on oil depot vulnerabilities in the Kuban area and potential Russian staging grounds for ballistic missile launches.
